evals.expected_output: Code path is systemic-only. Should dispatch the 4 systemic lenses (Architecture, Consistency, Robustness-systemic, Test-health), run Phase 2.5 blind-spots, then synthesize. Lens findings must be SYSTEMIC (multi-site patterns / structural properties / absences), e.g.: no parameterized-query / SQL-sanitization discipline across any DB call — process_order's INSERT and cancel_order's SELECT and UPDATE all f-string-interpolate untrusted values, an absence recurring across THREE DB call sites — a Robustness-systemic absence-across-sites (same shape as eval 2's path-traversal-across-3-methods); no input-validation discipline across the subsystem (validator validates nothing on payment_method/total/quantities; no boundary validates anywhere) — a Robustness-systemic absence-across-sites; no error-handling discipline at boundaries (event bus swallows nothing / propagates, DB calls unguarded) as a systemic pattern; god-object / coupling in OrderProcessor (Architecture). Single-reproduction instance bugs — the _processing_lock boolean race, inventory-not-released-on-cancel, the event-replay-duplicates bug — are NOT lens findings; with /delve absent they must surface under the 'Out-of-scope instance bugs (install /delve to triage)' stub (and would route to /delve via --bugs when present), never as lens findings and never silently dropped.
expectations: Announced running an audit on the order processing subsystem at start
Presented a scoping manifest of files in the subsystem before analysis (Phase 1)
Dispatched the 4 systemic lenses (Architecture, Consistency, Robustness-systemic, Test-health) plus a Phase 2.5 blind-spots phase — NOT an instance 'correctness' lens

Reported a SYSTEMIC Robustness finding: no parameterized-query / SQL-sanitization discipline across any DB call — process_order's INSERT and cancel_order's SELECT and UPDATE all f-string-interpolate untrusted values (an absence-across-sites recurring at all three DB call sites, enumerated in sites — treated systemically, the same shape as eval 2's path-traversal-across-3-methods, NOT a single-reproduction instance bug)

Reported a SYSTEMIC Robustness finding: no input-validation discipline across the subsystem — OrderValidator validates nothing on payment_method/total/quantities and no boundary validates anywhere (an absence-across-sites, enumerated in sites)

Reported a SYSTEMIC Robustness finding: no error-handling discipline at boundaries — OrderEventBus.publish propagates listener failures and DB calls are unguarded across the subsystem (a systemic pattern, not a single missing handler)
Reported a SYSTEMIC Architecture finding: god-object / coupling in OrderProcessor (DB, payment, inventory, event concerns concentrated in one class)

Did NOT report the _processing_lock boolean race, inventory-not-released-on-cancel, or the event-replay-duplicates bug as lens findings — these are single-reproduction instance bugs (the SQL interpolation, by contrast, recurs across three DB calls and is reported as a SYSTEMIC Robustness finding, not here)

Surfaced those single-reproduction instance bugs under the 'Out-of-scope instance bugs (install /delve to triage)' stub (or noted they route to /delve via --bugs), since /delve is absent — never silently dropped
Findings are organized by severity (Fatal/Significant/Minor) and grouped by theme
Did NOT modify any code — audit is find-and-report only
Offered to file findings as issues (individual or umbrella)

evals.expected_output: Code path is systemic-only. The 4 systemic lenses plus Phase 2.5 blind-spots should report SYSTEMIC findings: a systemic absence of path sanitization across every FileManager method that joins a user-controlled filename (save_file, get_file, delete_file — a multi-site pattern enumerated in sites); a systemic absence of authorization across the routes (no route checks that the caller owns user_id). Single-reproduction instance bugs — the specific decompression-bomb in ThumbnailGenerator.generate, the specific cleanup_temp_files deleting non-temp files, the specific missing quota check at the upload path — are NOT lens findings; with /delve absent they surface under the 'Out-of-scope instance bugs (install /delve to triage)' stub (or route to /delve via --bugs). The blind-spots / cross-cutting phase still runs.
expectations: Announced running an audit at start and presented a scoping manifest

Reported a SYSTEMIC Robustness finding: no path-sanitization discipline across the subsystem — every FileManager method (save_file, get_file, delete_file) joins a user-controlled filename without sanitization, allowing '../' escape (an absence-across-sites, enumerated in sites — not a single-site bug)

Reported a SYSTEMIC finding: no authorization discipline across the routes — no route verifies the caller owns the requested user_id, so any user can reach another user's files (a structural absence across the route surface)

Did NOT report the specific decompression-bomb, the specific cleanup-deletes-production-files defect, or the specific missing quota check as lens findings — these are single-reproduction instance bugs

Surfaced those single-reproduction instance bugs under the 'Out-of-scope instance bugs (install /delve to triage)' stub (or noted they route to /delve via --bugs), since /delve is absent — never silently dropped
Ran a blind-spots or cross-cutting analysis phase (the path-traversal + no-authz absences compound into a particularly severe systemic exposure)
Findings are severity-ranked with the most critical systemic issues first
Did NOT attempt to fix any code — presented findings for user decision
Synthesis phase deduplicated any overlapping findings from different analysis angles

evals.expected_output: Code path is systemic-only, and the skill must REFUSE to fix despite 'fix anything you find' (audit is find-and-report). The systemic lens finding here is the absence of locking discipline across all self.store mutations (get/set/invalidate/stats all touch the dict with no lock anywhere) — a Robustness-systemic absence-across-sites enumerated in sites. Single-reproduction instance bugs — the specific O(n) eviction in set(), the None-vs-miss conflation in cache_response — are NOT lens findings; with /delve absent they surface under the 'Out-of-scope instance bugs (install /delve to triage)' stub (or route to /delve via --bugs).
expectations: Did NOT modify any source code despite the user's request to 'fix anything you find'
Explicitly communicated that audit is a find-and-report skill — it does not make code changes
Still performed the full audit process — did not refuse to audit just because the user also asked for fixes

Reported a SYSTEMIC Robustness finding: no locking discipline across the subsystem — every self.store mutation (get, set, invalidate, stats) is unguarded despite multi-threaded use (an absence-across-sites, enumerated in sites — not a single-site lock bug)
Did NOT report the specific O(n) eviction or the specific None-vs-miss conflation as lens findings — these are single-reproduction instance bugs

Surfaced those single-reproduction instance bugs under the 'Out-of-scope instance bugs (install /delve to triage)' stub (or noted they route to /delve via --bugs), since /delve is absent — never silently dropped
Presented findings organized by severity
Offered to file findings as issues in the user's issue tracker

evals.expected_output: Code path is systemic-only. Systemic lens findings: in-memory-only session store with no horizontal-scale story (Architecture — a structural property of the subsystem); systemic absence of error handling on JSON decode/encode across handlers (Robustness-systemic — Login ignores the json.NewDecoder(...).Decode error AND Login/GetSession ignore their json.NewEncoder(...).Encode errors, a recurring across-sites absence enumerated in sites). Single-reproduction instance bugs — the int→rune string(rune(sess.UserID)) corruption in middleware, the nil-cookie panic in GetSession, the cleanup goroutine with no shutdown, the Session.Data-by-pointer concurrent-mutation defect, and the single missing Secure/HttpOnly/SameSite flags on the Login session cookie (a single-reproduction defect at one issuance site) — are NOT lens findings; with /delve absent they surface under the 'Out-of-scope instance bugs (install /delve to triage)' stub (or route to /delve via --bugs).
expectations: Announced running an audit and presented a scoping manifest

Reported a SYSTEMIC Architecture finding: the session store is in-memory only with no horizontal-scale / multi-instance story (a structural property of the subsystem — a single-site / whole-subsystem structural finding, NOT requiring 2+ sites; it carries a single representative site or the sites: [whole-subsystem] marker, and is gradeable as systemic on that basis)

Reported a SYSTEMIC Robustness finding: absence of error handling on JSON decode/encode across handlers — Login ignores the json.NewDecoder(...).Decode error and Login/GetSession ignore their json.NewEncoder(...).Encode errors (a recurring across-sites absence, enumerated in sites — not a single missing handler)

Did NOT report the string(rune(sess.UserID)) int→rune bug, the GetSession nil-cookie panic, the unstoppable cleanup goroutine, the Session.Data-by-pointer concurrent-mutation defect, or the single missing Secure/HttpOnly/SameSite flags on the Login session cookie as lens findings — these are single-reproduction instance bugs

Surfaced those single-reproduction instance bugs under the 'Out-of-scope instance bugs (install /delve to triage)' stub (or noted they route to /delve via --bugs), since /delve is absent — never silently dropped
Dispatched the 4 systemic lenses plus Phase 2.5 blind-spots (not a single-pass review)
Code findings include the mandatory sites enumeration with specific file references and evidence
Did NOT modify any code — presented findings for user decision
Synthesized findings — severity-ranked and theme-grouped
Offered to file findings as issues (individual or umbrella)
